title: Falang Minor

{{Quote|I don't hate the Tionese anymore. ''[…]'' But I don't trust them. No one in the Republic should ever trust them. They need to be watched, and my job is to watch them. All of us here on Falang watch them. And we always will.|Baragwin sense lattice imprinted by a Republic soldier|<ref name="Author's Cut" />}}
'''Falang Minor''', also known as just '''Falang''', was a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Thanium sector/Legends|Thanium Worlds]]. A [[Temperature/Legends|cold]] and [[Water/Legends|rainy]] world, it was among the speculated origin points of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Following the defeat of the [[Honorable Union of Desevro and Tion]] in the [[Tionese War]], a series of fortress worlds, including Falang Minor, were established from which [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] would watch over the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]]. After the end of the war, a [[Unidentified Republic soldier (Falang Minor)|Republic soldier]] served the [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Jedi watchmen]] for a period of twelve [[Standard year/Legends|years]], monitoring [[Starship/Legends|starships]], intelligence, and transmissions originating in the Tion Cluster. While serving on Falang Minor, the [[Republic soldier|soldier]] imprinted an audio-visual autobiographical account on a [[Baragwin sense lattice]].

==Description==
{{Quote|I don't like Falang Minor. It's cold and it rains all the time. There isn't anything that will grow or any beasts to tend.|Baragwin sense lattice imprinted by a Republic soldier|<ref name="Author's Cut" />}}
Falang Minor, also known simply as Falang, was a terrestrial<ref name="Author's Cut" /> [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="GatORW" /> located in the [[Falang Minor system/Legends|Falang Minor system]], a part of the [[Thanium sector/Legends|Thanium Worlds]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The world had a [[Temperature/Legends|cold]] climate with constant [[Water/Legends|rainfall]] and no animal or [[Plant/Legends|plant]] life.<ref name="Author's Cut" />

==History==
{{Quote|And now I've been here for 12 years. A Republic Guardsman, serving the Jedi watchmen. I monitor ship traffic, intelligence, transmissions — everything that comes out of the Tion.|Baragwin sense lattice imprinted by a Republic soldier|<ref name="Author's Cut" />}}
[[File:Tion_Cluster.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Jedi on Falang Minor, in the Thanium Worlds (left), watched the Tion Cluster (highlighted sectors in the center).]]
Falang Minor was among the worlds speculated to have served as the point of origin of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] at some point between [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]] and [[24,000 BBY]], with the organization ultimately tracing its roots to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] planet [[Tython/Legends|Tython]].<ref name="TEA" /> Upon the defeat of<ref name="Author's Cut" /> the [[Honorable Union of Desevro and Tion]]<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> in the [[Tionese War]] around [[23,900 BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> an arc of<ref name="EGTW" /> [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress worlds]] was established<ref name="GatORW" /> along the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Coreward]] boundaries of the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]], which eventually became a part of the Republic. The fortress worlds included<ref name="EGTW" /> Falang Minor, [[Haashimut]], and [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], the home of the Jedi Order at the [[Time/Legends|time]]. On those worlds, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] acted as the Republic's [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|watchmen]], monitoring the restless [[Star cluster/Legends|star cluster]]. Despite Falang Minor being a Republic fortress world,<ref name="GatORW" /> it nominally remained outside the borders of the government around [[22,000 BBY/Legends|22,000 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" />

Shortly after the end of the Tionese War, a [[Unidentified Republic soldier (Falang Minor)|Republic Guardsman]] was assigned to serve the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] watchmen on Falang Minor. While still a [[Youngling/Legends|boy]] during the war, the [[Republic soldier|soldier]] had survived a [[Tionese (group)/Legends|Tionese]] [[Attack on Okator VIII|attack]] on the celestial body [[Okator VIII]] that had destroyed their [[Unidentified farm (Okator VIII)|home]], after which the youth had enlisted with the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]]. The [[Soldier/Legends|soldier's]] duties on Falang Minor included monitoring [[Starship/Legends|starship]] traffic, intelligence, and transmissions originating in the Tion Cluster.<ref name="Author's Cut" />

After serving on Falang Minor for twelve [[Standard year/Legends|years]], the soldier imprinted on a [[Baragwin sense lattice]] an audio-visual account of the attack on Okator VIII. In the record, the soldier noted their displeasure at both the world they were stationed on—as opposed to the pastoral Okator VIII—and the nature of their duties, which gave them too many opportunities for introspection. Nevertheless, the soldier re-affirmed in the narration their distrust for the Tionese and their determination to continue watching them indefinitely, along with a conviction that all personnel on Falang Minor would similarly do so.<ref name="Author's Cut" />

==Behind the scenes==
Falang Minor was first mentioned in the [[2004]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] sourcebook ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''.<ref name="GatORW" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Falang Minor [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square R-6.<ref name="TEA" /> In [[2014]], [[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut]], a series of articles published on the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars Blog|Blog]] containing various cut content from the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', introduced a shortened version of the planet's name.<ref name="Author's Cut" />
